title: trifolium andinum
intro: Trifolium andinum, commonly known as the Andean clover or Queñua clover, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the high-altitude grasslands of South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Trifolium andinum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Trifolium andinum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
